landing.subtitle: Upload a PPT and AI will automatically generate lecture videos, scripts, and assessment questions. Track student engagement in real-time and maximize learning with RAG-based Q&A.
landing.whyIFLDesc: An optimized flipped learning experience for both instructors and students
landing.feature1Desc: Upload a PPT and an AI avatar will read the script to automatically generate lecture videos.
landing.feature2Desc: Embed lecture materials to provide accurate context-based answers to student questions.
landing.feature3Desc: Monitor learner viewing progress and response rates in real-time, with alerts for inactivity.
landing.feature4Desc: AI automatically generates formative and summative assessment questions based on lecture content.
landing.feature5Desc: A professor-exclusive dashboard showing attendance, accuracy, engagement, and costs at a glance.
landing.feature6Desc: Automatically translate scripts to multiple languages so international students can learn in their native language.
landing.step1Desc: Upload your existing lecture materials as-is
landing.step2Desc: Review and approve the AI-generated script
landing.step3Desc: Share the video link and learning analytics will start automatically
landing.ctaDesc: Create up to 2 lecture videos per month with the free plan
landing.copyright: © 2025 IFL Platform — Interactive Flipped Learning. All rights reserved.

answer.offTopicTitle: This question is outside the lecture's scope.
answer.offTopicBody: I couldn't find an answer in the lecture materials. Other EdTech chatbots will answer anything — ClassAuto is designed to keep students focused on learning.

mockAnswers.socialQ1: The 把 construction (把字句) places the object before the verb to emphasize that the object is being 'disposed of' or 'handled' in a specific way. It works when there's a definite object and the verb conveys a result, completion, movement, or change. e.g. 我把书放在桌子上 (I put the book on the table).
mockAnswers.socialQ2: A plain SVO sentence (我吃饭) describes the action itself, while the 把 sentence (我把饭吃了) emphasizes the result of the action on the object. The object must be definite, and the verb usually appears with a complement (result, direction) or the aspect marker 了 — bare verbs feel unnatural.
mockAnswers.naturalQ1: Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy: plants absorb sunlight and use CO₂ and water to produce glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆) and oxygen. It happens inside chloroplasts in two stages — the light reactions (light energy → ATP and NADPH) and the Calvin cycle (fixing CO₂ into glucose).
mockAnswers.naturalQ2: Chlorophyll is the pigment that absorbs light during photosynthesis. It mostly absorbs blue and red wavelengths and reflects green, which is why leaves look green. The absorbed light energy travels down an electron transport chain and becomes the chemical energy (ATP, NADPH) used to build glucose.

suggested.socialQ1: When should I use the 把 construction?

faq.title: Demo FAQ
faq.q1: Can I really try everything without signing up?
faq.a1: Yes — watching the video and chatting with the AI tutor are both available without an account.
faq.q2: Will my own lecture turn into a video like this?
faq.a2: Yes — a single PPT is all you need.
faq.q3: Is this the same screen students actually see?
faq.a3: It's identical to what students experience.
faq.q4: Can I try both demo videos?
faq.a4: One video per session, but "Try again" lets you switch to the other one.

meta.title: ClassAuto · AI flipped-learning platform
meta.description: Turn one PPT into a two-way lecture video. The video answers student questions and keeps attention with interstitial quizzes. A flipped-learning tool built by scholars, for scholars.

differentiators.title: Why ClassAuto isn't just another EdTech app
differentiators.subtitle: Guardrails are baked in so students stay on-task.

rag.title: RAG-bounded Q&A
rag.description: Off-topic questions are rejected by the AI (similarity threshold 0.7). The chatbot can't be used as a general search engine.

limit.title: Limit transparency
limit.description: ClassAuto runs on a flat subscription — never on usage-based pricing. Every screen surfaces your videos-per-month and active-learner count against the plan limit.

antiCheat.title: Anti-cheat by default
antiCheat.description: Interstitial quizzes, concurrent-playback limits, and macro detection are on by default. Video learning isn't decoupled from assessment.

studentData.title: Student data protection
studentData.description: Never used for ads. Auto-deleted on graduation, and students can request immediate deletion at any time.

stepsV2.title: Start from a single PPT
stepsV2.subtitle: All you do is upload the PPT and review the script. The AI pipeline handles the rest.

step1.title: Upload PPT
step1.description: Drop in your existing slides. Eight slides finish embedding in about a minute.

step2.title: Refine the AI script
step2.description: The AI drafts per-slide narration. You tweak a sentence or two via inline diff.

step3.title: Render and share
step3.description: TTS and avatar synthesis run automatically. Once done, you get a lecture link and QR code to share with students.

meta.title: Use cases — ClassAuto
meta.description: See how ClassAuto is used across languages, humanities, engineering, and social sciences.

hero.title: Will it work in my field?
hero.subtitle: If you have a deck and an instructor, ClassAuto produces a video lecture in about 12 minutes — regardless of discipline.

anchor.school: Kyonggi Univ., Dept. of Chinese Language and Literature
anchor.before: I wanted weekly current-affairs Chinese videos, but never had time to refresh the materials.
anchor.after: One slide + AI script + dual TTS = 12 minutes. A 14-week semester is now prepared in a single week.

social.school: Univ. A, Dept. of Political Science
social.before: Current affairs move fast and video material goes stale. I couldn't refresh weekly.
social.after: One slide + AI script = 12 minutes. Attendance went from 78% to 92%.

humanities.school: Univ. B, Dept. of Korean Literature
humanities.before: Classics need pre-reading for discussion to work. Pre-reading rates were low.
humanities.after: 5-minute pre-class video + Q&A bot to confirm comprehension. Discussion participation doubled.

engineering.school: Univ. C, Dept. of Computer Science
engineering.before: Equations and diagrams meant 4 hours per video.
engineering.after: Use the slides as-is, synthesize voice only — now 15 minutes per video.

lab.school: Univ. D, Dept. of Life Sciences
lab.before: Lab safety briefings repeated identical content every semester.
lab.after: One video, 6 semesters of reuse + auto-refreshed quizzes from incident data.

arts.before: Theory lectures lost student focus when videofied.
arts.after: 5-minute units + interstitial quizzes keep focus. Average accuracy rose to 82%.

meta.title: Student data trust — ClassAuto
meta.description: How ClassAuto handles student data, in plain terms.

hero.title: ClassAuto is a tool for students.
hero.subtitle: We don't use student data for ads. Data is auto-deleted after graduation.

ragLimit.title: RAG-scoped Q&A
ragLimit.description: The AI auto-rejects questions outside the lecture's own materials (similarity threshold 0.7). The chatbot is not a general-purpose search engine.

transparentCost.title: Transparent costs
transparentCost.description: We expose the actual API cost of each generated lecture to instructors. No hidden margins.

antiCheat.title: Anti-cheating
antiCheat.description: Interstitial quizzes, concurrent-stream limits, and macro detection are on by default. Video lectures are not separated from assessment.

studentData.title: Student data protection
studentData.description: Not used for ads. Auto-deleted after graduation, and any student can request immediate deletion.

items: We do not use it for ads

meta.title: Security — ClassAuto
meta.description: Infrastructure, access control, and incident response. Aimed at department heads and IT teams.

hero.title: Security info for institutional review
hero.subtitle: Runs on Vercel · Railway · Supabase, complies with Korean PIPA.

encryption.title: 1. Encryption
items: Per-column encryption for identifiers (student no., email)

access.title: 2. Access control
items: Instructors see only their own courses
Department heads (Phase 4) see only aggregate, never individuals

incident.title: 3. Incident response
items: Quarterly security audits, external review planned

downloads.title: Documents (on request)

contact.title: Security contact / vulnerability disclosure
